title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I've got my coffee, and I'm going to use a sleeve, so my hands don't get too cold.
Hmm, fire. This is going to give me the energy I need for work.

title: will / shall
examples: I usually like to eat in, but today I have to go back to work, so I'll do takeaway or takeout.
I'm also trying to cut back on caffeine, so I'll go for decaf.
I don't drink regular milk, so I'll choose the non-dairy option, which is oat milk.
And I'll add it to my cards. I'm ready to pay, so I'll place my order. No, thank you. I'm all set.
So this is my bill, and I will pay with my husband's card.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: At this cafe, you can place your order using a kiosk, so you don't even have to talk to a barista here.
Wow, it is so customizable here. You can add an extra shot of espresso or popping cream, which I'm guessing is whipped cream.
This is a tip. You can bring your husband's card. I don't have a loyalty card here, so I cannot earn points.
I'll also grab a straw, and some cafes even have bottles of syrup, so you can pump extra sweetener into your drink.
This one doesn't have a cure. If you have more than one drink, you can always ask for a carrier as well.

title: have to / must
examples: At this cafe, you can place your order using a kiosk, so you don't even have to talk to a barista here.
I usually like to eat in, but today I have to go back to work, so I'll do takeaway or takeout.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: This one doesn't have a cure. If you have more than one drink, you can always ask for a carrier as well.
